title: State Denies Parental Recognition Based on Family Structure

violations.source: Article V Section 4.1, Section 4.3, and Section 7.2
violations.summary: State officials refuse to recognize the parental status of a married same-sex couple raising a child born during the marriage solely because of the sex, gender, or family structure of the parents. Article V protects family formation and parental recognition from unjustified government interference and bars discriminatory classifications affecting family status.

satisfied_duties.duty: restore lawful parental recognition and enjoin enforcement of the discriminatory family-status rule
satisfied_duties.outcome: restored parental recognition, barred enforcement of the discriminatory family-status rule, and held that family decisions may not be denied legal effect because of the sex, gender, or sexual orientation of the parents

ai_summary.primary_violation: State officials refuse to recognize the parental status of a married same-sex couple raising a child born during the marriage solely because of the sex, gender, or family structure of the parents. Article V protects family formation and parental recognition from unjustified government interference and bars discriminatory classifications affecting family status.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties
